title: FuFirE BaZi Golden Case
description: A single golden reference case for BaZi pillar calculations. Every case must declare its source_type so test_golden.py can distinguish externally-verified oracles from engine-derived baselines.

id.description: Stable identifier for the case; kebab_or_snake_case.

birth_local.description: Local civil time of birth in ISO 8601 (no timezone suffix; the zone is given separately).

source_type.description: ENGINE_BASELINE: expected values were produced by the engine itself; useful only for regression detection. EXTERNAL_ORACLE: verified against a domain-authoritative external source (citation required). DOMAIN_REVIEW_REQUIRED: case is included but expected values are not yet verified; tests must treat it as a known gap.

source.description: Free-text source note. For EXTERNAL_ORACLE entries this MUST cite a verifiable reference (author, book, almanac, ephemeris, court ruling, etc.).

time_standard.description: Time standard used to produce the expected pillars. TLST is opt-in / Phase 1+.

day_boundary_scheme.description: Whether the day pillar rolls over at civil midnight or at 23:00 (start of Zi hour).

ruleset_id.description: Ruleset under which the expected pillars were verified, e.g. 'standard_bazi_2026'.

ephemeris_id.description: Ephemeris that produced the expected values, if applicable.

verification.description: Metadata about how (and when) the case was verified.

tolerance_seconds.description: Optional time tolerance for boundary-adjacent cases (LiChun, Jieqi, Zi). When set, the case asserts that pillars are correct anywhere within this many seconds of birth_local.
